[[Casey McLean]] (cousin) }} }} '''Tiaki Chan''' (born 15 June 2000) is a {{rlnt|France}} international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]], on loan from the [[Wigan Warriors]]. ==Personal life== Chan's father [[Alex Chan (rugby league)|Alex]] is a former [[Catalans Dragons]] and [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand international]] player. His brother [[Joe Chan|Joe]] is also a professional rugby league footballer. Chan's cousins [[Casey Mclean]] and [[Jesse McLean]] are also professional rugby league footballers. ==Club career== ===Catalans Dragons=== In 2022 he made his [[Catalans Dragons]] debut in the [[Super League]] against the [[Leeds Rhinos]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP37733464|title=Super League: Catalans Dragons v Leeds Rhinos|publisher=BBC|date=30 July 2022|accessdate=1 August 2022}}{{dead link|date=September 2025|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} Chan played 13 games for Catalans in the 2023 Super League season but did not play in their [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}} ===Wigan Warriors=== On 26 June 2023 it was reported that he had signed with [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] for the 2024 season on a three-year deal.{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=26 June 2023|accessdate=10 October 2023|title=Wigan Warriors sign France international from Catalans Dragons on long-term deal|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-warriors-sign-tiaki-chan-catalans-dragons}} ====Hull F.C. (loan)==== On 7 May 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]] on loan{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=7 May 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Wigan Warriors boss confirms duo loans as he provides Mike Cooper, Sam Walters latest|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-warriors-boss-matt-peet-confirms-sam-eseh-tiaki-chan-loans}} ====Salford Red Devils (loan)==== On 31 January 2025, it was announced that Chan had joined the [[Salford Red Devils]] on a season long loan from the [[Wigan Warriors]].{{cite web|url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2025/01/31/chan-joins-salford-on-loan|title=Chan joins Salford on loan|date=31 January 2025|publisher=Wigan Warriors}} ====Catalans Dragons (loan)==== On 19 February 2026 it was reported that he had signed for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]] on an initial one-month loan.{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 Feb 2026|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Wigan Warriors loan forward to Catalans Dragons for first-team opportunities|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-warriors-loan-catalans-dragons}} ====Toulouse Olympique (loan)==== On 27 March 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]] on loan until the end of the 2026 season.{{cite web|website=Toulouse Olympique|date=27 Mar 2026|author=Allan McKenzie|title=SQUAD NEWS : FRENCH international Tiaki Chan joins TOULOUSE until the end of the season|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/squad-news-french-international-tiaki-chan-joins-toulouse-until-the-end-of-the-season/}} ==International career== Chan made his {{rlnt|France}} début on 29 April 2023 in the 64-0 defeat to {{rlnt|England}} at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]].{{cite web|url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/fixtures/1189/england-64-0-france |title=England 64 0 France |website=European Rugby League |access-date=25 July 2024}} ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/fiche-657-chan-tiaki/ Catalans Dragons profile] {{Toulouse
